name: Save the Cat
description: Blake Snyder's 15-beat structure for compelling screenplays and novels.

beats.name: Opening Image
beats.prompt: A visual that sets the tone, mood, and stakes. What image captures your protagonist's 'before' state?

beats.name: Theme Stated
beats.prompt: Someone states the theme or lesson—usually to the protagonist. What's the central question or moral?

beats.name: Setup
beats.prompt: Introduce the protagonist's world, flaws, and what's missing. Plant seeds for later payoffs.

beats.name: Catalyst
beats.prompt: The event that sets the story in motion. Life will never be the same after this moment.

beats.name: Debate
beats.prompt: The protagonist hesitates. Should they accept the challenge? What doubts or fears hold them back?

beats.name: Break into Two
beats.prompt: The protagonist makes a choice and enters the new world of Act Two. There's no turning back.

beats.name: B Story
beats.prompt: Introduce the secondary story—often a love interest or mentor who helps explore the theme.

beats.name: Fun and Games
beats.prompt: The promise of the premise! Deliver what the audience came to see. What makes this story unique?

beats.name: Midpoint
beats.prompt: A false victory or false defeat. Stakes are raised. The protagonist commits fully.

beats.name: Bad Guys Close In
beats.prompt: External pressures tighten. Internal doubts grow. The team may splinter. Things fall apart.

beats.name: All Is Lost
beats.prompt: The lowest point. Often includes a 'whiff of death'—literal or symbolic. What's lost forever?

beats.name: Dark Night of the Soul
beats.prompt: The protagonist wallows in hopelessness. They must find strength to continue. What do they realize?

beats.name: Break into Three
beats.prompt: A fresh idea or revelation! The protagonist synthesizes everything and knows what to do.

beats.name: Finale
beats.prompt: The final battle. Old world vs. new world. The protagonist executes the plan and proves they've changed.

beats.name: Final Image
beats.prompt: The opposite of the opening image. Visual proof of the transformation. What's the 'after' state?
